articles.title: Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders
articles.description:
Boston College senior Merideth Greene is honoring firefighters and supporting the Last Call Foundation's mission.

The post Boston College senior running Boston Marathon to support first responders appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston College senior Merideth Greene is running the Boston Marathon on April 17, 2023.
Greene supports the Last Call Foundation, which aids first responders and their families.
Her efforts raise awareness and funds for local firefighters and emergency personnel.

articles.title: Secret Service agent assigned to Jill Biden accidentally shoots himself in leg at airport
articles.description:
Biden was not in the area when the agent was injured during a “negligent discharge” of his firearm Friday morning, Secret Service spokesman Anthony Guglielmi told The Associated Press.

The post Secret Service agent assigned to Jill Biden accidentally shoots himself in leg at airport appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Secret Service agent accidentally shot himself in the leg at an airport on Friday.
Agent was assigned to protect Jill Biden; she was not present during the incident.
Incident raises concerns about firearm safety protocols among security personnel in Boston.

articles.title: After a decade as a Yale hospital janitor, she is now a doctor there
articles.description:
“I still can’t believe it,” said Shay Taylor-Allen, who landed a residency at Yale New Haven Hospital, where she was born and spent most of her adult life on the cleaning staff.

The post After a decade as a Yale hospital janitor, she is now a doctor there appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Shay Taylor-Allen transitioned from janitor to doctor at Yale New Haven Hospital.
She worked as a janitor for 10 years before starting her residency.
Her journey inspires Boston residents pursuing careers in healthcare despite challenges.

articles.title: Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily
articles.description:
The Chinatown staple has served hungry late-night diners for decades. Its current owners want to retire, and new owners are taking over.

The post Peach Farm is closing — but only temporarily appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Peach Farm in Chinatown is temporarily closing for ownership transition.
Current owners are retiring after decades of service to late-night diners.
New owners plan to reopen, ensuring continuity for local patrons.

articles.title: Senate approves funding for TSA and most of Homeland Security, but not immigration enforcement
articles.description:
Bipartisan agreement funds most Homeland Security operations, sidestepping ICE and border enforcement as Democrats and Republicans remain at odds.

The post Senate approves funding for TSA and most of Homeland Security, but not immigration enforcement appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate approved funding for TSA and Homeland Security on March 27, 2026.
Funding excludes immigration enforcement, reflecting ongoing partisan disagreements.
Boston residents may see changes in local security operations without ICE involvement.

articles.title: Minnesota to host ‘No Kings’ flagship rally, headlining Springsteen amid tensions over ICE and war
articles.description:
Organizers designated the Minnesota rally, at the State Capitol in St. Paul, as the flagship event. Last June’s event drew an estimated 80,000 people.

The post Minnesota to host ‘No Kings’ flagship rally, headlining Springsteen amid tensions over ICE and war appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Minnesota's State Capitol will host the 'No Kings' rally on June 1, 2024.
Bruce Springsteen will headline the event, following last year's 80,000 attendees.
Boston residents may see increased activism related to ICE and war tensions.

articles.title: Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force
articles.description:
A new unit to target SNAP, MassHealth, and election-related fraud, with a public hotline, has been established.

The post Mass. U.S. attorney launches benefit, voter fraud task force appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts U.S. Attorney launched a task force to combat SNAP and election fraud.
The task force includes a public hotline for reporting fraud incidents.
Local residents may see increased scrutiny on benefit programs and voting integrity.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to ‘Today’ on April 6 after mother’s disappearance
articles.description:
“I can’t come back and try to be something that I’m not. But I can’t not come back, because it’s my family.”

The post Savannah Guthrie to return to ‘Today’ on April 6 after mother’s disappearance appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Savannah Guthrie will return to 'Today' on April 6 after her mother's disappearance.
Guthrie expressed the struggle of balancing personal and professional responsibilities during this time.
Her return may affect local viewers' engagement with the show amid ongoing family concerns.

articles.title: United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it?
articles.description:
Relax Row will be available in 2027.

The post United to offer couch-style economy seating — would you try it? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: United Airlines will introduce Relax Row seating in 2027 for economy passengers.
Couch-style seating aims to enhance comfort for travelers flying from Boston.
Boston residents can expect a new travel option that may improve long-haul flights.
